topic: Decision Making to Improve Operational Performance
subject: business-studies

items.q: What is a brand in marketing?
items.correct: A combination of name, design, symbols and associations that distinguishes a product and creates customer perception and loyalty — more than just a logo.
items.misconception: A brand is just the logo on a product.
items.random_wrong: A brand is the company that makes a product.
items.why: Branding is the sum of perceptions, not just visual identity. Coca-Cola the brand is much more than the script logo — it is associations, taste expectations, advertising history.

items.correct: The external macro-environment: Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal and Environmental factors affecting a business.
items.misconception: PESTLE analyses the internal strengths and weaknesses of a business.
items.random_wrong: PESTLE stands for Profit, Earnings, Sales, Trade, Loans, Equity.
items.why: Students confuse PESTLE (external) with SWOT (internal + external). PESTLE is about the wider environment a business cannot control; SWOT mixes internal and external.

items.correct: Holding minimal stock and receiving inputs only when needed for production — reducing storage costs but increasing vulnerability to supply disruption.
items.misconception: Just-in-time means delivering products to customers exactly on time.
items.random_wrong: Just-in-time means finishing work right before the deadline.
items.why: JIT is about INPUT stock to production, not output delivery to customers. Students misread the term as a delivery promise; it is an internal stock control philosophy.

items.q: Modigliani and Miller's irrelevance theorem was published in:
items.why: It's 1958. “1948” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: Cost of capital. “Annual cost calculation” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did Modigliani and Miller publish their capital structure theorem?
items.why: Exact answer: 1958 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
